summary:
Thiotepa has no documented role in current osteosarcoma chemotherapy guidelines based on available literature; instead, high-dose ifosfamide is supported—particularly for relapsed or refractory cases—as an effective alternative therapy. Therefore, it should not be recommended for treating osteosarcoma. 

\boxed{\text{No}} The summary focuses only on clinically significant findings from the retrieval process regarding thiotepa’s efficacy and inclusion in standard protocols, avoiding irrelevant details such as drug mechanisms or off-label uses outside direct relevance to osteosarcoma management. It directly addresses the core issue by highlighting absence of supporting data while affirming established alternatives like ifosfamide within appropriate patient populations.

Final Summary: There is no evidence supporting the use of thiotepa in osteosarcoma treatment; high-dose ifosfaidra is preferred for relapsed or refractory disease per guidelines. \boxed{\text{No}}
